skill.name: gws-docs
skill.description: Use the gws CLI to inspect, create, read, and update Google Docs.
skill.summary: Operate Google Docs through documented gws CLI commands.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and no malicious intent was found. The reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, HTTP library, and path traversal alerts are Markdown or text false positives. The remaining real risk is intended use of an external gws CLI that can read and write Google Docs after authentication.

medium_findings.title: Google Docs Access Through External CLI
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use gws docs commands and Google Docs API resources. This is expected behavior, but authenticated use can read, create, or modify Workspace documents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands and API methods directly support Google Docs read and write operations. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it depends on authenticated user permissions.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but SKILL.md only contains Markdown inline code and bash examples for the gws CLI. No executable Ruby code or dynamic shell construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and the flagged lines are code fences or inline command references. There is no Ruby source, command interpolation, or user-controlled shell execution in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Relative Skill Links Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts point to relative links for neighboring skill documentation. They reference ../gws-shared/SKILL.md and ../gws-docs-write/SKILL.md, not arbitrary filesystem access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative paths are visible Markdown documentation links and prerequisites. No evidence shows traversal of user data or unrestricted file reads.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And HTTP Library Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts on lines 3 and 24 and the HTTP library alert on line 32 do not correspond to cryptographic code or Python HTTP imports. They are false positives from ordinary prose and table text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain a description, a Markdown table header, and API prose. No cryptographic function, hashing call, Python import, or outbound HTTP implementation appears in the file.

content.value_statement: Teams need a reliable way to inspect and update Google Docs from AI coding tools. This skill documents the gws commands for Google Docs resources, schemas, and write operations.
actual_capabilities: Shows the base gws docs command pattern for Google Docs resources and methods.
Lists the documents resource methods batchUpdate, create, and get.
Points agents to gws schema for required parameters, types, and defaults.
Links to the companion write helper for appending text to documents.
Directs agents to shared Google Workspace authentication and security guidance.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI to be installed and authenticated before use.
Does not include full parameter examples for every Google Docs API method.
Write operations depend on the authenticated account permissions.
Shared authentication guidance is stored in a neighboring skill file.

use_cases.title: Prepare Document Automation
use_cases.description: Inspect Google Docs method schemas before building document creation or update workflows.

use_cases.title: Update Team Documents
use_cases.description: Append or batch update content in shared Google Docs through authenticated CLI commands.

use_cases.title: Review Document State
use_cases.description: Fetch the latest version of a Google Doc before summarizing, editing, or validating it.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Google Docs Help
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws-docs skill to show me how to inspect available Google Docs resources and methods before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Get A Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws-docs skill to determine the schema for getting a Google Doc, then explain the required parameters before running anything.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws-docs skill to inspect the create method schema and prepare a Google Docs creation command for a draft titled Project Notes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan A Batch Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws-docs skill to inspect batchUpdate, identify the required request structure, and propose a safe update plan for an existing document.

output_examples.input: Show me how to discover the required parameters for a Google Docs method.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the gws docs help command, then recommends inspecting the specific method schema before building parameters.

output_examples.input: Prepare to create a blank Google Doc named Weekly Review.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that the create method accepts a title and returns the created document, then asks for confirmation before running the command.

output_examples.input: I need to append text to a document.
output_examples.output: The assistant routes the task to the companion write helper and checks authentication guidance before preparing the update.

best_practices: Inspect the method schema before building command parameters.
Use the least privileged Google account that can complete the task.
Confirm document identifiers and write operations before applying changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run write commands before checking the schema and target document.
Do not use a personal account for broad workspace automation.
Do not paste sensitive document content into prompts unless required.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It documents how to use the gws CLI with Google Docs resources and methods.

faq.question: Does it install the gws CLI?
faq.answer: No. The gws CLI must already be available in the environment.

faq.question: Can it write to Google Docs?
faq.answer: Yes. It points to batch update and companion write workflows when the authenticated account has permission.

faq.question: Can it read private documents?
faq.answer: Only documents available to the authenticated Google Workspace account can be accessed.

faq.question: Why should I inspect schemas first?
faq.answer: Schemas show required parameters and data shapes, which reduces failed commands and unintended document changes.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium risk notice because it uses an authenticated external CLI for document access.
